model_justification: Competitive landscape overwhelmingly uses subscription with volume tiers (Loop $155-340/mo, AfterShip $11-239/mo, ReturnGO $121-297/mo). The idea's secondary marketplace component adds a transaction-based revenue stream on top. Hybrid model (subscription for core returns automation + 15% take-rate on resale GMV) mirrors Optoro's enterprise approach but targets mid-market, and is validated by the subscription dominance among direct competitors plus marketplace models from adjacent players like B-Stock and ReturnPro.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Mid-to-high positioning within the $121-340/mo mid-market range justified by high pain intensity (Problem score 79, pain_intensity=high). $199/mo undercuts Loop ($155-340/mo at Essential-Advanced) while exceeding AfterShip ($11-239/mo) at the mid tier. Additional marketplace commission (15% on resale GMV) provides upside. Price validated against 6 competitors with discoverable pricing.

unit_economics.churn_source: SaaS SMB-midmarket benchmark: 3-5% monthly for SMB, 1.5-3% for mid-market (Vitally 2025, WeAreFounders 2026 industry report). Used 3.5% as blended estimate for mid-size ecommerce merchants with moderate switching costs (Shopify/WooCommerce integration, configured return policies, customer-facing portal).
unit_economics.margin_source: SaaS gross margin benchmark 75-80% (CloudZero 2025, GSQuaredCFO 2026). Blended down to 75% to account for marketplace component which has lower margins (60-70%) due to payment processing and logistics coordination costs.
unit_economics.cac_source: eCommerce SaaS CAC benchmark: $274 average, SMB $200-300, mid-market $250-350 (WeAreFounders 2026 industry report). Used $300 for mid-market targeting via content marketing + Shopify app store channel (lower CAC channel within the range).

cac_plus_20.assessment: Even with 20% higher CAC ($360), the ratio remains excellent at 17.8x with sub-2-month payback — unit economics are highly resilient to acquisition cost increases.

churn_plus_20.assessment: At 4.2% monthly churn, LTV drops to $5,339 but ratio stays at 17.8x — the low CAC of ecommerce SaaS provides a large buffer against retention challenges.

price_minus_20.assessment: Even at $239/mo blended ARPU (competitive with ReturnGO mid-tier), LTV/CAC remains 17.1x with under 2-month payback — significant pricing flexibility exists.

model_precedents.evidence: $53.3M revenue in 2024, up from $31M in 2023. $176M total funding (Series B $50M Jan 2023). 3,500+ merchant customers. Valued at $340M post-money.

model_precedents.segment: SMB to mid-market e-commerce (11K customers, 100K+ businesses)
model_precedents.evidence: $85M revenue in 2024, profitable. $67M total funding. Suite includes returns, tracking, shipping. Grew mainly through word-of-mouth and Shopify integration.
$11.5M seed funding. 1,500+ Shopify stores. Acquired by Global-e Online in July 2025 — validated by strategic acquirer in cross-border ecommerce.

model_precedents.segment: Enterprise retailers (IKEA, Best Buy, American Eagle)
model_precedents.evidence: $200M total funding. Acquired by Blue Yonder Aug 2025. Processes 100M+ goods. Combines returns management with resale marketplace (OptiTurn routes to BLINQ/BULQ). Closest precedent to the hybrid returns+resale model.
Acquired by UPS for $465M (from PayPal). 10,000+ physical Return Bar locations. Validated returns management as strategically valuable to logistics majors.
$277M ARR. Serves similar mid-size Shopify merchant segment. Demonstrates subscription SaaS model viability for ecommerce tools at scale.

assumptions: ARPU of $299/mo is blended: $199/mo subscription + ~$100/mo estimated marketplace commission. Marketplace revenue assumes 500 returns/mo, 30% unsuitable for resale as new, $20 avg resale price, 15% take-rate, with conservative early adoption factor (basis: competitive pricing benchmark + industry return rate data).

Monthly churn rate of 3.5% based on SaaS SMB-midmarket benchmarks (3-5% SMB, 1.5-3% mid-market). Mid-size e-commerce merchants have moderate switching costs due to platform integrations (basis: Vitally 2025, WeAreFounders 2026 benchmarks).

CAC of $300 based on ecommerce SaaS industry benchmark ($274 average, $250-350 mid-market range). Assumes content marketing + Shopify app store as primary acquisition channels (basis: WeAreFounders 2026 industry report).
Gross margin of 75% based on SaaS benchmark 75-80%, blended down for marketplace component at 60-70% margins (basis: CloudZero 2025, GSQuaredCFO 2026 benchmarks).
Marketplace take-rate of 15% based on standard marketplace commission rates; resale pricing at ~50% of retail value based on returned goods disposition industry norms.
